title: Clipse Secures Five Grammy Nominations, Including Album of the Year
text: Clipse has earned five nominations at the 2026 Grammy Awards, marking a significant achievement for the Virginia Beach duo's first collaborative album in over 15 years. The hip-hop pair received nominations for Album of the Year, Best Rap Album, Best Rap Performance for Chains & Whips featuring Kendrick Lamar and Pharrell, Best Rap Song for The Birds Don't Sing with John Legend and Voices of Fire, and Best Music Video for So Be It. Their project Let God Sort Em Out is a 13-track offering that has been widely praised, with producer Pharrell Williams contributing to the album, representing a major reunion for the group. Pusha T expressed determination about winning hardware, stating We need to take something home for this, emphasizing the group's confidence in the album's strength. Malice took a broader perspective, noting the duo's artistic validation matters most, saying We have already been validated with our art form and the music that we put out. The Album of the Year category features all first-time nominees including Kendrick Lamar leading with nine total nominations, Tyler the Creator, Bad Bunny, Justin Bieber, Sabrina Carpenter, Lady Gaga, and Leon Thomas.
